body.title: ExtraSuite Docs
body.subtitle: The AI-native workflow for Google Workspace
body.p: ExtraSuite is an open-source CLI tool built for AI agents. It provides a secure, token-efficient, and auditable way to read and write Google Workspace files — Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Forms — using a simple pull / edit / push workflow.
Instead of navigating the complex Google APIs directly, agents work with plain files on disk. ExtraSuite handles the translation between those files and the live Google document.
The core workflow has four steps:
body.li: Pull — Download the Google file and convert it into a local folder of readable files.
Edit — Modify the local files directly. No API knowledge required.
Diff — Preview what changes will be sent to Google. No network calls.
Push — Apply the changes to the live Google document via the API.
body.p: ExtraSuite brings a git-inspired pull/diff/push model to all Google Drive file types. Every pull captures a pristine snapshot so the diff engine knows exactly what changed.
Each employee's agent gets a dedicated 1:1 service account. The agent only has access to files explicitly shared with it. All changes appear in Google Drive's version history under that identity.
Agents never see raw tokens. The ExtraSuite server issues short-lived credentials using OIDC and domain-wide delegation. Tokens are cached locally and refreshed automatically.
Files are serialized into compact, LLM-readable formats — XML for Docs, TSV for Sheets, SML for Slides — so agents consume fewer tokens than working with raw API JSON.
body.li: extradoc — Google Docs with full paragraph, table, and list support
extrasheet — Google Sheets with formula and formatting support
extraslide — Google Slides with shape and layout support
extraform — Google Forms for reading and editing form questions
Gmail — Draft creation via delegated OAuth
Calendar — Event management via delegated OAuth
